column5: It is a strange week in the camp. The atmosphere is muted after the attack by... those creatures. People are eating by themselves, and the usual cheer around the fires at night is replaced with sullen drinking and early nights.
More than a few fetches are sent after deserters.
Yet you awake refreshed and ready for you next mission on the seventh day, with the usual orders to report to Agata's tent for your briefing.
What do your characters do?

rolls out of his bed as normal. starts to grab his weapons for the start of the next assignment. In silence, he strides out towards Agata's tent. As he approaches he coughs and clears his throat by the tent entrance.
grumbles.
Better be some coin in this mission - old Quinn can't help but notice that we didn't get any of those swine coin none, and no mistake!
gets up and sits on the edge of the cot... fighting for motivation through the fuzz in their head.
follows after Biron, continuing to grumble as he goes.
grabs their pack and heads to Agata's when they hear the Biron's cough. They take a second to be grateful they chose a tent close to Agata's.
As per usual you are greeted at Agata's tent by Corporal Loth. Always tall, always incredibly handsome, and always unfailingly rude, he opens the tent for you with a slight sneer of his perfect face.
doffs his non-existent cap at Loth as he passes, smiling nervously.
ignores Loth.
walks in, giving Loth a single nod.
You enter the dark confines to find... Agata is not there!
moves into place to the right of Agata's desk, fold his arms and looks into the distance as though waiting.
stays silent and to attention, despite the missing Sergeant and apparently a missing group mate as well.
lets his hand drop to the fine looking basket hilt of his rapier.
squats down onto the dirt floor to wait.
The silence becomes unbearable as the awkward moment seems to draw on forever, begging someone to break it.
Finally, the terrible moment is broken by Agata storming into the tent.
sits in her chair with a harrumph and starts angrily filling her pipe with tobacco.
shoots up off the ground, startled.
eyes you all with a sharp look.
nods, smiles and makes a poor effort to salute.
lights her pipe with a flint, puffs on it, inhales deeply, and blows a large cloud of pipe smoke into your faces.
Well, that's a blasted thing!
Burn it!
coughs.
You'll notice we're a man short... it appears Corporal Ba'Raknul has been requisitioned by the musketeers regiment.
Pompous arses!
What?
gives Biron the side-eye.
Turns out his marksmanship on... that night turned heads.
One of my best fetches!
slams her fist on the table.
flinches.
I don't... understand.
looks at Agata but doesnt say anything.
sighs.
Indeed, big boots to fill - if ya take m'meaning some, ma'am.
He's not on the mission with us?
By the Father! No. Not ever.
chews on their lip.
rubs at his chin.
So, just wondering like, and nothing meant by it like and no mistake... but, what's the pay like in the muskateer regiment?
eyes narrow on Quinn.
You thinking of rolling on me too, Private Quinn?
looks to the floor.
Yeah, well was just me mind a-wandering like. Got no complaints here none, no ma'am. None whatsoever. Couldn't be 'appier none...
coughs a moment.
No ma'am, we are here to do our duty.
gives Quinn a sideways glance and goes quiet again.
stares long and hard at Biron.
As the most senior ranking officer, that puts you in charge of this unit, Corporal Biron.
May the Mother help us.
scratches at their neck.
gives a single nod and grunt in acknowledgement.
stifles a chuckle.
That means you need to curb that temper of yours. No more "frenzies" involving senior officers.
Understand?
nods again at Agata.
Yes ma'am.
I'd say very good but my heart just isn't in it today.
Very well, onto your mission.
scrabbles around the books and papers on her desk until she finds the right document.

This is an important one. The Jinibian Concern, as some of you may recall, are one of our more generous clients. More generous than the Royalists, who have yet to pay many of the various writs they owe us.
eyes light up at the mention of 'generous clients'.
There are four-hundred-and-fifty gold crowns to divide amongst you, but it requires some delicacy if you are to be successful. Think you can handle delicacy?
eyes Biron in particular.
looks to Biron.
laughs.
For 150 gold crowns, I'd not only handle delicacy but show her a night on the town!
clears his throat and looks embarrased.
If ya take me manner of speaking some..
nods.
It shall be done.
sniffs.
What you do on your own time is your business, Private Quinn. But I get your point.
turns to Loth.
But this one has us scratching our heads. The Concern appear to have a vendetta against the Locksmith's Guild, of all people.
peers around the tent.
Any idea why that might be?
leans in, interested.
No.
stares at Agata silently.
Could there be some... erm, illegal keys being distributed some like? Perhaps those with less than honourable intent got skeleton keys to the Concern's concerns... if you take me meaning some.
ponders this for a moment.
Could be... could be.
What do they want us to do?
Apparently the Bank of Palanthis is increasing the security of their vaults. A new lock is being constructed by the Locksmiths.
The Concern want you to find out where this lock is being made and sneak their man in to sabotage the lock.
Are you all following this?
nods.
grins.
Sabotage as in 'make it easy to bypass'? Sounds like a crooked business and no mistake. Count me in.
So... the Jinibian Concern is concerned that the Bank of Palanthis' security may be too good?
It's difficult to say what the Concern's ambitions in this matter are. I've never met folk who talk and write so much, but say so little.
But I can't imagine a few guildsmen will give seasoned fetches like you much pause, so I imagine it's easy money.
furrows their brows... concerned.
Do we have any other details.
frowns and looks to Biron and Orin.
Any of yous read some? Ain't somethin' I've ever bothered with. Sounds like I kinda should have bout now.
looks at Quinn, shocked.
I suppose I could teach you?
ignores Quinn.
Find lock, Put their man in. No mess. It shall be done.
As always, Corporal Biron, I pay you to find out the details. You're to meet your contact Femi-Descends-From-Orun at the Green Dragon in the Chalk Quarter.
gives a grunt of acknowledgement and a nod.
Any other questions?
shakes their head.
rubs at his chin.
Do we know if there are other interested parties in this lock?
I imagine the Goldsmiths Guild would have a stake in such a lock, given it's their bank. Otherwise, I have no idea.

Clear as crystals I be thinking - but I'm wondering to meself, where is the headquarters of this 'ere guild? Seems a good enough place to search if any. Or do you be thinking that their man may have clues to this lock's whereabouts ma'am?
I'd be surprised they'd be constructing the lock there given it's obvious nature as a target for every thief in the Midlands.
nods and doffs his cap, smiling stupidly.

Very true and wise, ma'am. But ya know how these folk like to write stuff down and like. And well, me friends here being learned folk like, they might just find something that tells them where the lock is in that there key house. Just a thought ma'am. Don't mind old Quinn none.
nods.
Apologies, Private Quinn. Indeed, it would make an ideal place to start investigations.
This is soooooooooo not a mission for Biron.
Talky and sneaky.
Heh.
You are such a ninja!
Ha ha the tank no talky 😛
I've been known to creep up on people in a fight. but thats my best haha.
pauses a moment thinking.
So it's likely their will be heavy security surrounding this.
Now, I have a busy morning of pipe smoking and fuming to be getting on with.
Dimissed!
bows clumsily and backs out of the tent, half tripping on a guide rope in the process.
nods and leaves, but turns around to wait for the others.
gives a grunt and a nod and walks out of the tent.
We can't just wander around asking questions in Dismas. Forget that we're wanted there, Agata asked for discretion. We'll need to pose as someone else...
The father help us if we run into Rothaniel.
They got their bad guys there. Just dont pick up a dog.
pauses silently. and looks down at his burned scar on his arm and thinks.
rubs at his chin.
Ain't too sure Quinn is much good at trying to be someone he's not like. It's the rugged good looks see - makes me stand out some!
You're not understanding me, Biron. No one can know that Black Griffin is snooping around. And if the same people are asking questions all around this lock, that will be suspicious too.
looks up at Orin from his arm.
Chances are we'll need more than one cover.
We have been given our orders.
Exactly, Biron.
What else did you think our orders meant?
We go to Dismas. Quietly if possible. Speak with our contact.
pauses and thinks a moment, not ready for this charge in any case.
Two places we should look at are the shop itself and the people who maybe interested in it themselves, like other thieves.
Femi-Descends-From-Orun at the Green Dragon in the Chalk Quarter.
nods at Biron.
Aye, that would be our first port of call so to speak... lets set off some and discuss plans on the way. The mother will guide us. Probably.
nods at Quinn.
casts about him and whispers.

I'm kinda thinking that you be the best folk for the job Orin. I'll not tread on Biron's 'ere's feet none if ya take my meaning, but I be thinking we should follow ya lead, and no mistake. If that's ok with you Ser Biron?
Sorry, what?
looks at Quinn, perplexed.
whispers.
I'm thinkin' you be best to take charge. And I be knowing it ain't my place none to be suggesting the like. That's Biron place... but I'm just speaking me mind.
guffaws nervously.
grins nervously.
looks at Orin.
You do have the appropriate and required skills for this job.
What are you saying? You think I should... what he said?
nods.
I'm a kinda thinking you could command Orin to take command, so to speak..
I'm your senior so it still falls on me, but a good leader in anything knows whose strengths to rely on at what time. You don't go to war with your weakest people.
You should take point on this.
grins at Biron.
Where's that violent buffoon we came to know and love?
grins nervously.
And not meaning nothin by it none...
'Biron ignores Quinn''... do you have that hotkeyed? 😛
Lol.
Ha ha ha.
Birons a brute, but he ain't stupid, lol.
ignores Quinn and gives Orin a stern look.
Don't make me regret this though.
guffaws and kicks at the dirt.
I mean... if you think so.
shakes themself a little.
Well, let's get on the road and talk about it more, then.
snaps to attention.
Aye aye!
I just don't want to go into Dismas without some sort of idea how we're going to do this.
double checks the state of their pack and weapons, then heads toward the South Gate.
wipes his nose with his hand.
Is it far? Wonder if we will get rations for the journey like? Seems a tad rich to expect us to use our own coin some...
stares again at the brand on his arm that he shared with Ba and thinks for a while. Quietly.
looks at Quinn, rolls his eyes, grunts, and walks away.
The party sets off for the South Gate, passing from the fetch's camp, circling around Larke's House, and passing through the row of tents, stalls, and vagabonds that is the followers camp.
Hold on.
is still looking at his brand on his arm.
stops and looks at Biron.
Last time we went Dismas.
We went in secret.
Should we do that again?
sniffs.
If ya were secret last time, they won't recognise ya none this time, right?
We didn't end up that secret, Quinn... that's the problem.
looks at Quinn and then to Orin.
growls.
Fuck it.
carries on walking.
Wait!
holds his hand up.
Well, ya may have a point some. I 'ave collected some old clothes and the like in me travels some. If ya think it will do us some good, we could change our clothes and such?
We went in a crate last time didnt we.
Ba sorted us out a way out.
Not the last time.
I don't think.
Must've been the time before.
I thought we entered through the sewers.
I didnt think it was sewers cos Ba was scared of them due to the rat folk.
Yeah, he warned us all to hell, but went anyway.
That's when we fell through the old bath floor.
Yes, we should go in secret, Biron. But I wasn't thinking of going in through the sewers this time...
pauses.
looks horrified.
And ruin me fine clobber?
Rather, I thought we would go in the front... under a guise?
cringes, waiting.
smiles.
Well, as I say. I 'ave some old clothes and erm, some make up I accidentally borrowed from this fine lass I got to knowing down in Faro...
grunts and nods.
You go forward.
looks to the sky.
Oh, she was a beauty some, and no mistake. T'ill the paint came off at least. Perhaps old Quinn could be just as tempting with her magic paint and fumes, if ya take my meaning.
Just as long as me and Biron don't look like us.
nods.
looks to Orin.
I didn't steal it much! Just wanted a memento of money well spent...
looks around.
Lets do this out of sight.
What is it on us that usually marks us as Black Griffin?
Its a pin or something isnt it.
Just that you look like mercenaries armed and all, and you bear the black griffin on your shields or armour somewhere.
Well, not both of us... I had a funny idea we could go in as guild members from the north? Looking to get some tips from the great guild in Dismas?
grunts. unhappy about the situation.
If it works, it works.
That should get us free access to the guild in general, no?
isn't really listening,just staring at the other members of the black griffin.
rubs at his chin.
Must say, being a southerner, I don't know much about the north. Not sure I'd not crack under the pressure some. And I guess they'd do that writing stuff to mark who's who and what not?
It's harsh. Deadly. Danger from elements and creatures a like.
Home.
Alright... perhaps you and I, Quinn, we can be hired escorts? War is dangerous for travellers, no?
chucks out a short laugh.
rolls their eyes.
Yes Biron, we know you're impenetrable.
picks at his nose with his thumb.
Dunno, I be thinking maybe Orin here is the potential guild member, and us, the hired muscle. That way Orin does the talkin' and we just have to look pretty.
winks at Biron.
nods at Quinn.
That works better.
Alright. But we've got to get these crests off us, and I've got to look less prickly. We can manage that on the way?
looks at his shield.
looks to his own shield.
Hmm... yeah, this could be a problem some. Like I say, I have this ladies make-up... rouge and blush and the like. Not sure it will cover wood and steel though. It's more for skin.
casts about him.
Not that I've tried it none...
Just cover it in paint.
No, I don't think we'll need the makeup, exactly. Maybe just a little to change the shape of Biron's and my faces.
Oh! Perhaps Walter can forge us a guild introduction?
rolls his eyes.
raises an eyebrow.
Would anyone in the camp have such an introduction?
Well, I had one for the Bowyer's guild once. I could tell him what it looked like and he could change the words to suit better?
rubs his chin.
And are the guilds affiliated in some way? I means, would an intro to one look much like an intro to anothers some?
I honestly couldn't say.
I mean, presumably they signed right? You know like you rich folk do to make the words ya'own?
It's not really an official letter, exactly. Just from my mentor who knew the guild in Dismas. Oh, but he had a stamp, I suppose. We might be able to get one in Dismas?
pulls his hand from his nose and wipes it down his trousers.
Mind if I sees it some?
Ouch, sore subject.
glowers.
I don't have it anymore. Not since they sent me to Black Griffin.
grunts.
Are we going to leave or just stand here.
shrugs.

Well fair enough friend, old Quinn wouldn't be able to make much sense of it, and no mistake. But perhaps we are on to something. If we can find someone who is a member of this guild in this town, we could... erm... borrow their one. Or any official looking letter or such.
keeps walking.
Yes, I think we've got enough to get us started? I can give Biron my armour and you two need to remove the crests from your things. But we can enter Dismas with what we've planned so far.

As the party passes through the followers' camp merchants, beggars, and misfits all attempt to attract your attention. A large burly man with a thick accent prepositions the party with an offer of “men, woman and more besides.
pulls back a tent cover to reveal a crowd of bored looking folk.
Ivar has only the best
Across the way a small, bird-like crone dressed a long black dress and black veil offers to read your fortunes.
And close by a familiar-looking boy, missing an arm, sullenly begs for change.
rushes to the boy.
It's you!
The Lively boy!
frowns.
You?
grunts.
startles and looks back at Orin without any sign of recognition.
grunts.
We brought him here. Pig men attack.
What are you doing here?
grunts. He looks a little bit scared and wary, but holds out his good hand for alms.
sniffs, distraught.
This can't be how you live?
looks at the boy with his normal look of contempt.
rubs the stump of his arm and looks confused.
Come on, we have things to do.
looks at Quinn and starts to walk away.
waits on Orin.
ignores Orin and the boy. He looks on ahead with a darkened face, marching onwards to start the trip to Dismas.
I-- I can't stay. I'm sorry. But here...
takes out 5 gold pieces from their pack.
You must get somewhere better than this. It's too dangerous.
looks even more confused, perhaps even about to cry. He nods and takes the coins.
Anywhere... maybe... Haverstead? It's southwest of here. Ask for Gil. He will help you, I think.
I'm sorry, I've got to go. We just can't have saved your life for this...
the boy simply looks as on as you walk on, hand in a tight fist around the coins. It is not clear if he even understands.
catches up to Biron, but continues to look back on the boy as they walk away.
So it is with a heavy heart, on a grey, sunless day, the party sets off down the road to Dismas, the foulest city in all the Land.
stops.
We should buy him some gear and food, shouldn't we?
he has coin. He must learn to survive. If he does, he'll be stronger for it.
grumbles under his breath.
And if he don't, he'll be dead for it.
Then he dies.
rubs their eyes and walks backwards a bit before running to catch up.
